title: These 8 Stocks Yield Up to 8.3% and Their Payouts Could Soon Rise
author: Brett Owens
description: The article highlights eight dividend-paying stocks with yields up to 8.3% that are expected to increase their payouts soon. These companies have demonstrated strong earnings growth and maintain low payout ratios, suggesting room for dividend increases. The stocks span various sectors including construction, healthcare, aerospace, HVAC, telecommunications, tobacco, investment management, and energy infrastructure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Dividend has doubled in three years with 33.3% boost last year; expected continued bottom-line growth; dividend announcement expected mid-September
Two-decade uninterrupted dividend increases; doubled payout over five years; 20% increase in 2025 despite profit pullback; double-digit profit rebound expected; announcement early August
23% net income growth in 2025; 33% growth expected in 2026; pays out less than 10% of earnings; likely summer dividend hike announcement
Net income nearly doubled in 2025; dividend 77.7% higher year-over-year; multiple raises per year since 2021; strong growth expected; announcement late July
Evolved into cellular powerhouse; dividend program started 2023 with 57% increase; 2.3% yield still lower than competitors but rapidly closing gap; announcement mid-September

Dividend King with 50+ years uninterrupted increases; pivoting to high-growth smokeless products expanding 25% annually; strong pricing power; sure dividend hike expected late August

title: Don't Buy SpaceX Until You Consider These 2 Aerospace and Defense Stocks With 10% EPS Growth
author: James Halley
description: The article argues that Howmet Aerospace and TransDigm Group are more attractive aerospace and defense investments than SpaceX, citing their strong EPS growth profiles, high-margin aftermarket businesses, and disciplined acquisition strategies. Both companies benefit from aging aircraft fleets requiring maintenance and possess significant pricing power through proprietary products and sole-source supplier relationships.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates 540% EPS growth over five years, 71% YoY EPS growth in Q1, strong aftermarket business expansion (48% YoY growth in spare parts), emerging AI/data center opportunity with 39% gas turbine revenue growth, and aggressive acquisition strategy with $1.8B CAM acquisition expected to add $275M revenue and $60M EBITDA.

Company shows 270% EPS growth over five years, 11.6% YoY EPS growth in Q2, exceptional pricing power with 80% sole-source supplier status and 90% proprietary products, industry-leading 52.6% EBITDA margins, strong aftermarket growth (16% YoY), and aggressive capital deployment with $905M in buybacks and $960M acquisition of Stellant Systems.

Stock declined from opening day closing price of $160.95 to $147.11 shortly after IPO, suggesting weak post-IPO performance and investor disappointment despite initial hype around Elon Musk becoming a trillionaire.

title: Micron’s Record Sales Show the Memory Bottleneck Is Real
author: Louis Navellier
description: Micron Technology reported record sales and earnings, demonstrating that the memory bottleneck is real and benefiting the memory and SSD industry. The author also discusses defense contractors developing advanced aerospace technology and recommends selling cryptocurrencies due to Bitcoin's significant decline and a strengthening U.S. dollar.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Record sales and earnings announcement demonstrates strong demand for memory products and validates the memory bottleneck thesis, supporting future growth for memory-related companies.
Listed as a primary defense contractor of interest due to development of advanced aerospace technology, though sentiment is based on speculative future potential rather than concrete fundamentals.
Mentioned as a defense contractor of interest but no specific positive or negative developments discussed; included for speculative technology development potential.
Listed as a defense contractor of interest but no specific positive or negative developments discussed; included for speculative technology development potential.

title: Micron Earnings Could Be the Grand Finale for AI and Memory Stocks
author: Louis Navellier
description: Memory and AI-related technology stocks are experiencing a buying opportunity as Micron Technology prepares to announce record earnings on Wednesday. Analysts have revised consensus earnings estimates up 4.8%, and the author expects continued strong performance through 2029 due to a 3-year order backlog in AI and data center stocks. Recent market corrections are viewed as temporary dips in an otherwise bullish trend.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company expected to announce record sales and earnings; analyst consensus estimates revised up 4.8%; positioned as the grand finale to earnings season with strong fundamentals
Mentioned as a primary defense contractor of interest for potential advanced aerospace technology development, but no specific earnings or performance data provided

title: These 2 Industrial Stocks Have Had a Strong Year. Buy Them and Hold Forever.
author: Jack Delaney
description: Howmet Aerospace and Generac Holdings are highlighted as long-term industrial stock buys following strong year-over-year performance. Howmet benefits from the growing aerospace market projected to double by 2035, while Generac is capitalizing on data center backup power demand. However, both stocks have elevated valuations and may face short-term volatility after significant recent gains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong commercial and defense aerospace revenue growth with favorable long-term market projections (aerospace market expected to nearly double by 2035). However, elevated P/E ratio of 55 and 700% five-year gain suggest limited near-term upside and vulnerability to earnings misses.

Significant momentum in data center segment with 10% commercial/industrial division growth and strong CEO commentary on accelerating data center demand. Nearly 100% one-year gain demonstrates market confidence, though forward P/E of 25 reflects optimism that requires continued execution.
